ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH has strong annual results and remarkable growth in luxury
content: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) (+11.05% to 528.48 euros) is the leading player in the stock market after revealing robust annual results despite a disturbed environment. In the wake of this performance, luxury values are on the rise. All LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)'s activities show sustained organic growth of their sales with the exception of wines and spirits. The group achieved two-digit organic growth in Europe, Japan and the rest of Asia. In 2024, its current operating result was at a level comparable to that of 2023, at 22.8 billion euros by adjusting the 2023 data for a better comparison. The current operating margin is stable compared to 2023. The group's net share is 12.5 billion euros. Its sales amount to €84.6 billion, an organic growth of 7% compared to €2023 (79.1 billion). LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) signs a remarkable performance of fashion and leather goods activity with an organic growth of 8% of its sales (€42 billion). Perfume and cosmetics activity also shows a particularly strong dynamic: organic growth of 7% of sales. Watches and jewellery activity achieves an organic growth of 5% of its sales in 2024. However, its sales of wines and spirits decreased by 3% in organic because of a base of comparison and a high stock level. But LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) points out that the year 2024 was marked by an increase in champagne. Stifel: "good cost control". Commenting on these annual results, Stifel remains at the Achat on the title LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) now its price target at a comparable level. "In terms of profitability, investors were afraid of a failure, but good cost control helped to beat the consensus. The group's operating margin has exceeded expectations. The Financial Director confirmed that the group wants to maintain the current level of profitability by 2025 thanks to new cost control measures. LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) announced that it will propose a dividend of 13 euros per share at the general meeting. A dividend deposit has been distributed. The balance will be paid.

ticker: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC)
title: LVMH: Revenues down by 10% in the first quarter of 2024, worried investors.
content: LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) posted a significant decrease in its consolidated turnover in the first quarter of 2024, recording a decrease of around 10% in comparison with the previous year. This performance is mainly due to a marked decline in some of its iconic brands, particularly in Asia-Pacific. The turnover of some of its brands is expected to fall by almost 20% in comparison to March 31st. Moreover, the combined effect on the published turnover of the positive contribution from recent acquisitions and the negative impact of exchange rates is estimated to be around -1% to -2%. LVMH MOËT HENNESSY LOUIS VUITTON SE (MC) will announce its turnover for the first quarter of 2024 on April 23rd after the stock exchange. This announcement leads to a fall in the price of LVMH share, currently at €868.11, in a context where luxury values are particularly scrutinized. Investors are concerned about this downward trend that affects the entire sector.

title: L'ORÉAL : The analysis office confirms its advice on the purchase with a course target of 300 €
content: The analysis office confirms its advice on buying and its target price of €300 after the publication of the figures of the 3rd quarter. After the performance of the 1st quarter, we are recording our forecast of sales of L'ORÉAL by +2%-3% for a profit per share (BPA) of +5%-6%, with prospects that should be noted in the 2nd quarter from a slight decline to potential growth, according to us, on the basis of a greater precision of the supply of raw materials and the expansion of the range of hair care products in the United States. 'We believe that the quality of the ORÉAL product portfolio remains underestimated, with many innovations being developed this year to gradually strengthen confidence, although the conclusion of some research and development projects in the 3rd quarter seems uncertain, but probably remains in favour of the risk/reward ratio of the action: +4%-6% against -1%-2%', adds the analytical office. With a current price at €262.13 and annual revenues constantly increasing (€41.18 billion in 2023), the ORÉAL seems well positioned to continue to show solid growth in the coming quarters. The firm's strong financial performance, with a net profit of €6.18 billion in 2023, strengthens investors' confidence in L'ORÉAL's ability to maintain a positive growth path.

title: L'Oréal: Return on net income and revenue in 2023, but a dividend maintained.
content: L'Oréal announces a net income share of the group in decline from 6.5% in 2023 to 6.18 billion euros, for a revenue decrease from 3.2% to 41.18 billion euros. The world leader in cosmetics invokes a decrease in volumes "mainly on sales of non-strategic products" and a negative exchange effect. It will propose the distribution of a dividend of 4.80 euros per share paid on 5 June. In 2024, the evolution of the sales and profitability of the group will depend "on the level of demand in the various markets, the launch of new products and the evolution of the world economic situation", announces L'Oréal.

title: Hermès International : Le Luxe Resistes and Folds Records with a Business Number of 15.17 Billion!
content: HERMÈS INTERNATIONAL (RMS): The trend is up in the short term. The title of the French luxury giant is back in colour. The title continues to grow in depth, even though the general environment is in distrust, driven by solid financial results. Indeed, the company's latest annual revenues show a remarkable increase, with a total turnover of 15.17 billion euros in 2024, and a net profit of 4.60 billion euros over the same period. What scenario is to be preferred to accompany this growth? by Daniel Cohen de Lara, President of AFATE.

title: Hermès at the top of the loss in luxury: analysts are looking down on their price targets!
content: HERMÈS INTERNATIONAL (RMS) : The luxury sector is in the red, Hermès in the lead. The group's fundamental performance and low-denominated stock market performance have led to a series of poor performances despite its solid figures. As a result, the financial analysts have lowered their price targets due to lower than expected revenue growth and net profit. As an indication, the current price of € 255.18 is considered high in relation to the results of the last financial years, notably with a net profit of € 4.6 billion for a turnover of € 15.17 billion as at 31 December 2024. What are the new objectives to be met?

title: "TotalEnergies: indicators for the first quarter of 2024 show promising stability and growth"
content: TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE) has published its main indicators for the first quarter of 2024. Oil production is expected to be stable, above 2.45 million barrels of oil equivalent per day, despite the actual disposal of unspecified assets in the fourth quarter of 2023, driven by the increase in LNG production and the start-up of new projects. Integrated Power's results are expected to rise thanks to the growth of the activity. Moreover, the oil group indicates that its European refining margin indicator increased sequentially to $71.7 per tonne in the first quarter of 2024, compared with $52.6 in the fourth quarter of 2023. The results of the Aval will reflect the increase in refining margins, with the refinery utilization rate in the first quarter of 2024 being broadly stable compared to the fourth quarter of 2023. Given the low volatility in the first quarter of 2024, the results of oil and LNG trading are expected in the historical average. This positive trend is expected to contribute to a strong financial performance for the company, which has already demonstrated its ability to generate significant revenues, with a total turnover of $218,945 billion in 2023 and a net profit of $21,384 billion.

title: TotalEnergies: the high-voltage general meeting of climate activists and shareholders
content: TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE) is preparing for a new general assembly under tension, which the demonstrators of the climate cause would be well shaken Friday at the moment when shareholders have to decide on the climate issues and the renewal of the CEO Patrick Pouyanné. For the first time in two decades, the fourth world oil major and largest French company by profit, which celebrates this year a new stage of its development, will hold its annual Grand Mass at home, in its 48-storey tower in La Défense, rather than in a room in the middle of Paris. Objective: to avoid "immobilize a district of Paris", explains the group, as it had happened during a tumultuous 2023 edition, marked by warm-ups between demonstrators and police officers on the outskirts of the hall hosting the event. A year later, the pressure did not fall. In the streets or in court, the group remains under the fire of critics of climate advocates, who accuse it of aggravating global warming and harming biodiversity and human rights, as a result of its activities in gas and oil. Organizations have explicitly called for a push to the AG. Among them, the Extinction Rebellion movement, which requires the "abandonment" of flagship projects in Uganda/Tanzania, Mozambique and Papua New Guinea, and, as advocated by the International Energy Agency, "the cessation of any investment in new fossil projects". Between "300 and 600" protesters are expected, according to a police source. "A big device" is planned, the source said, which expects like last year to face confrontations between ecologists and shareholders. Faced with the "risk" of disturbances to public order, the police prefect of Paris took a decree prohibiting undeclared demonstrations in a perimeter around the Tour Coupole. On the agenda, shareholders, present or online, will be called to vote on the climate strategy of TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE), some investors also demanding a more ambitious energy transition. Tuesday, 22% of Shell's shareholders rejected its climate plan, a relatively high proportion. Last year, at TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE) a purely consultative resolution from militant shareholders had even won 30.4% of the votes. It called on the company to align its greenhouse gas emission reduction targets with the 2015 Paris Agreement, to limit global warming to +1.5°C compared to the pre-industrial era (the world is already at about 1.2°C). The President and CEO of TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE), Patrick Pouyanné, spoke at a session of the Qatar Economic Forum in Doha on 15 May 2024. This time, no consultative resolution is expected. A coalition of shareholders claiming 0.9% of the capital demanded in vain, until the court, a non-binding resolution aimed at "discontinuing the cumulation of the functions of president and general manager" held by Patrick Pouyanné, to keep him in the position of general manager. An unthinkable choice for the board of directors, attached to the "strategic stability" of the company that earned $21.4 billion in profits in 2023, after $20.5 billion in 2022, and 16.03 billion in 2021, and will propose to shareholders to renew the CEO for a fourth term. The hot leader of the company for the past 10 years will preside this meeting on the background of controversy after his statements on a possible relocation of the main listing of the Paris group to the New York Stock Exchange. From the comments that triggered the lightnings of the political class, from Bruno Le Maire to Emmanuel Macron. As to appease the criticisms, he mentioned on Thursday in Le Figaro a "translation error": he did not want to talk about a main quotation on Wall Street but about a transformation into classic shares of the securities already traded in a form reserved for foreign companies. Mr Pouyanné pointed out that the Americans buy more shares than the Europeans, more constrained by rules of sustainable investment. The CEO repeats it, TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE) is "the oil group most involved in the energy transition". A third of its investments are devoted to low-carbon energy, 95% of which is renewable electricity. But in September, the think tank Carbon Tracker estimated that "only" the Italian oil company Eni had emission reduction targets "potentially" aligned with the Paris agreement. TOTALENERGIES SE (TTE) came second before Repsol and BP, far ahead of the Saudi Aramco or the American ExxonMobil.

title: Sanofi : Barclays raises its target course and confirms its recommendation for purchase
content: SANOFI (SAN) is appreciating slightly in Paris, following Barclays' confirmation of its recommendation 'weight online' on the title, with an adjusted price target to reflect the new growth prospects. The analyst has revised his forecast of operational expenditure for SANOFI, integrating the latest trends in the health sector. Sales estimates for certain key drugs, such as those in the SANOFI portfolio, have been adjusted to anticipate prolonged growth until 2033, thus benefiting from long-term visibility. In addition, the results forecasts for the first quarter have been updated to reflect current market conditions. Barclays is now aligned with the consensus of analysts and plans performance for 2024 above SANOFI's targets, demonstrating renewed confidence in the company's ability to deliver strong results.

title: Sanofi announced a decrease in net income and revenue in the first quarter of 2024.
content: Sanofi reveals a net decrease of 12.1% to 556 million euros in the first quarter of 2024, i.e. 4.44 euros per share, as well as an EBITDA down from 3.2% to 1.33 billion euros, i.e. a slight increase in EBITDA margin from 0.3 points to 23.1%. Revenue decreased from 5.5% to 11.07 billion euros, with a slight increase in volumes, but a negative price effect reflecting mainly the pressure on the prices of certain medicines and the still unfavourable market conditions in certain therapeutic areas. Sanofi has focused on strictly managing its operations and making progress in carrying out its main projects in progress in order to reap the first benefits in the spring, says its CEO Paul Hudson. The health group confirms its target for the year 2024 of an estimated EBITDA of between EUR 10.5 billion and EUR 11.5 billion, 'depending on the level of demand recovery, and with a more weighted seasonality on the second part of the year'.

title: Breeze Airways orders an additional 10 A220-300 aircraft from Airbus SE.
content: We are pleased to announce that a leading airline, Breeze Airways, has placed an additional 10 A220-300 aircraft on Airbus SE. This new order brings Breeze's total firm orders for this type of aircraft to 90, making it one of Airbus SE's leading customers for the A220. Breeze Airways has also confirmed its commitment to use a fleet entirely composed of Airbus A220 for its commercial operations by the end of 2024. It should be noted that Breeze Airways had received the delivery of its first Airbus A220 in December 2021 and is currently operating 20 aircraft across the United States. This new order demonstrates the airline's confidence in Airbus SE's products and confirms the company's position as a leader in the aviation sector. Thanks to its innovative and efficient product range, Airbus SE continues to meet the needs of airlines and their passengers. Airbus SE's revenues have continued to grow in recent years, with a total turnover of 69.23 billion euros in 2024, while the company's net profit has also increased to 4.23 billion euros in 2024.

title: "Airbus SE: The downward trend continues, attention to oblique resistance"
content: Shareholders of Airbus SE (AIR), We inform you of a worrying situation regarding the evolution of the price of our share. Of the last 5 sessions, the price of the Airbus SE share has shown a notable decrease of -5%, showing an unencouraging graphic structure. This pattern is not isolated, since we have observed since mid-October a trend to significant increases of +5 or +6% in 4 or 5 sessions, followed by equivalent and rapid relapses. It is crucial to closely monitor the technical situation, especially the slash resistance (basis) which currently stands at around 555 €, close to the current price. The test of the level of support at 540 € could be in sight, if the downward trend continues. We invite you to remain vigilant in this situation and to follow the recommendations of our management and analysis teams to adjust your strategies accordingly. This follow-up will be maintained and additional information will be communicated to you if necessary.

title: Schneider Electric in negotiations to acquire Preligens, specialist in artificial intelligence for 220 million euros.
content: SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU), a leader in the field of energy and industrial automation, announces that it is in the process of discussions to acquire 100% of Preligens' capital, a specialist in artificial intelligence (IA) dedicated to the energy and industry industries 4.0. Preligens' value is estimated at 220 million euros. This acquisition would represent for SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU) "a unique opportunity to add AI capabilities to its offer at the state of the art, while accelerating the roadmap of its digital transformation, in particular that applied to Industry 4.0". With its international presence, SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU) would also support the development of Preligens in the world, particularly in the US market. Preligens, based in France, achieved a turnover of 28 million euros in 2023 and employs 220 employees, including 140 engineers for the research and development division. Founded in 2016, Preligens offers proven AI-based solutions to analyze complex industrial data. Preligens' acquisition would speed up the adoption of AI within the group, thus optimizing our agility and efficiency. With multiple applications, this contribution would represent a major step in the development of our activities in energy and industry 4.0, and would also allow us to deploy digital inspection methods to enhance the quality and safety of industrial operations. This operation is subject to the usual regulatory approvals, as well as to the procedures applicable in terms of information and consultation of Preligens staff representatives. Its completion is expected in the third quarter of 2024. "We are convinced that the acquisition of Preligens will be an important step for our company, allowing us to increase our expertise in artificial intelligence and to strengthen our position in the energy and industry market 4.0", says a spokesperson for SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU). SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU) achieved a turnover of 35.9 billion euros in 2023 and employs more than 29 000 people worldwide. This acquisition is part of the group's growth and innovation strategy, which has already invested significantly in the research and development of intelligent energy and industrial solutions.

title: Schneider Electric forecasts a stable turnover for 2024-25 despite an expected decline in the first half of the year.
content: SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU) announces a stable turnover for the financial year 2024-25 at constant scope and exchange rates, with potential for an increase if activity in the energy sector recovers earlier than expected and with a strong seasonality effect during the financial year. The turnover in the first half of 2024-25 is expected to decrease by about 15% in organic and annual terms, before a rebound in the second half of the year, taking into account the current trend in annual revenues of EUR 35,902 billion in 2023-24. The EBITDA margin for the financial year is expected to fall to about 35%, similar to that of the previous year when the profit margin was strong despite a decrease in revenues. For the financial year 2023-24, SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC SE (SU) confirmed that it expected a decrease of around 10% in organic sales, a performance slightly lower than the EUR 35,902 billion of the previous year, but an EBITDA margin of about 34% is maintained, as indicated above.

title: "AIR LIQUIDE and Dunkirk LNG win EU support for the Artagnan CO2 capture and export project"
content: AIR LIQUIDE is proud to announce that the European Commission has decided to support the D'Artagnan project, an innovative transport and export infrastructure for CO2, initiated in partnership with Dunkirk LNG. This ambitious project aims to significantly reduce greenhouse gas emissions in the Dunkirk basin. As part of this project, AIR LIQUIDE will contribute to the creation of a specialized pipeline for the transport of CO2 from the capture sites to a terminal located in the port of Dunkirk. From this terminal, CO2 will be liquefied and then loaded on LNG vessels to be exported. This project represents a major investment estimated at more than 400 million euros. It is noteworthy that D'Artagnan is the first CO2 infrastructure project in France to benefit from the financial support of the European Union. As part of the CEF-E (Connecting Europe Facility - Energy) funding programme, the project is eligible for a grant of more than EUR 160 million, subject to the actual implementation of the project. This initiative is perfectly in line with the strategy of AIR LIQUIDE to reduce its carbon footprint and promote more sustainable energy solutions. By partnering with Dunkerque LNG for this innovative project, AIR LIQUIDE reaffirms its commitment to the energy transition and the fight against climate change. The recent financial results of AIR LIQUIDE, with a total turnover of EUR 27.058 billion and a net profit of EUR 3.306 billion as at 31 December 2024, demonstrate the financial strength of the group. This solid financial situation allows AIR LIQUIDE to invest in strategic projects such as D'Artagnan, thus contributing to a more sustainable future. AIR LIQUIDE is delighted with this opportunity which not only strengthens its position as a leader in the industrial gas sector but also illustrates its active role in reducing CO2 emissions and promoting sustainable energy practices.

title: The Paris Stock Exchange is falling, driven by Air Liquide's disappointing results.
content: The Paris Stock Exchange ended in decline by 0.42% on Wednesday, following a session marked by a decline in the title of AIR LIQUIDE, whose results were considered disappointing, despite a record net profit. The leading index CAC40 fell by 32.15 points to 7.596.36 points. On Tuesday, it had increased by 0.65%, supported by a surprise rebound of industrial orders in Germany. The session was marked by the sharp drop of AIR LIQUIDE which abandoned 3.50% to 542.52 euros. The French industrial gas giant achieved in 2023 a record net profit of 3,3061 billion euros, up 7.4% over a year. This growth is in the wrong direction because it benefits from a favourable comparison against the result of last year. Its adjusted profit thus fell by 2.5% to 3,078 billion euros, compared to 2022. The fourth quarter results were also slightly lower than the analysts' forecasts surveyed by Bloomberg. On the macroeconomic level, "there is always a form of attentism on the markets as regards the expectations of monetary policy" of the central banks, comments Nathalie Benatia, macro economist of Bnp Paribas AM. "The most interesting element for economists is the interview of Isabel Schnabel (member of the Executive Board of the European Central Bank, NDLR) at the Financial Times, who wants to convince observers that the ECB will not lower its rates as quickly" as anticipated by the markets, continues the analyst. Inflation in the services sector is still high, a resilient labour market and geopolitical tensions are prompting prudence as regards a further fall in rates expected by the markets in April. AIR LIQUIDE also announced that it plans to revise its growth forecasts for 2024 downwards due to more difficult market conditions than expected. The group confirmed that its turnover 2023 amounted to EUR 27,058 billion, down 2% from 2022.

title: ESSILORLUXOTTICA decreased after HSBC degradation, but long-term prospects remain strong
content: ESSILORLUXOTTICA (EL) recorded a decrease from 2.22% to 70.49 euros, one of the largest market declines, following a deterioration of HSBC. In its analysis note, the bank decided to buy to conserve on the title of the French-Italian telescope while lowering its price target. "We lower our profit estimates for the financial year 2024-2025 by 2% due to a lower EBIT margin than expected last year", explains the bank to justify its change of recommendation. The visibility and short-term growth of ESSILORLUXOTTICA is somewhat more blurred and the shares have performed well (up from the lowest) , but HSBC expects better growth in the medium term, fuelled by its constant product innovations (myopia, transition lenses, hearing aids). Regarding organic sales growth in the first quarter of 2024, HSBC believes that for the multinational, this period could be the least dynamic quarter of the year. The bank is thus based on organic growth of 4.9%, and an increase at constant exchange rate. In addition, ESSILORLUXOTTICA tends to increase prices only through the product mix, but it seems that the company will carry out a little more pure price increases this year, which are expected to materialize towards the end of the second quarter of 2024. In addition to this deterioration of HSBC, this Tuesday, ESSILORLUXOTTICA announced new developments in its strategic expansion, thus strengthening its position on the market. Investors can be pleased with the positive long-term dynamic of the company given its latest solid results with a total revenue of 26508000000.0 and a NetIncome of 2359000000.0.

title: ESSILORLUXOTTICA: Turnover 2023 down 4.5% despite an increase in net profit.
content: ESSILORLUXOTTICA (EL) announced a decline in its overall turnover in 2023 from 4.5% to 25.31 billion euros, despite a net profit which continues to increase. Its sales in Europe, which represent 40% of turnover, declined by 2.1% and those internationally by 6.3%. One year earlier, the company had already experienced a decrease of 0.5% in its turnover, but had seen its net profit increase by 15.5%. Sales of optical products, which represent 80% of turnover, show a decrease of 3.2%, compared with a decrease of almost 10% for sales of bezel products. With 15 closures of stores recorded during the year, ESSILORLUXOTTICA has only 850 of them. The company is shaken like the whole sector of the optics by the inflationary wave which started in 2022. The group will present on 12 March its annual results 2023 and its medium-term transformation plan. This announcement could impact the price of the action which is currently at €79.17, after having reached record levels of net profit in recent years, notably with a net profit of €2.359 billion in 2024, according to the latest figures available.

title: BNP Paribas transfers its online banking subsidiary to focus on its core business.
content: BNP Paribas has entered into an exclusive agreement with the Danish group Ageras to divest its online banking subsidiary for entrepreneurs and independents. On the Stock Exchange, the bank's share loses 0.85% to 519.25 euros within a slightly growing sector in Paris. No financial details have been given about this transaction. BNP Paribas had developed this activity internally in recent years to meet the growing needs of professionals. This divestment project is helping to simplify BNP Paribas", explained the banking group. "It allows the group to refocus on its retail banking network in France, as well as on specialized banking services, to continue to grow in the customer segment of professionals and improve its financial performance." The project is expected to be completed in the first half of 2025. BNP Paribas' strategic roadmap, presented in 2023, aims to "shape a simplified, more synergistic and efficient model while strengthening the Group's capital." With annual revenues of 65.87 billion euros in 2024 and a net profit of 11.688 billion euros, BNP Paribas is confident in its ability to strengthen its solvency ratio and continue its growth path. At the same time, BNP Paribas recently announced several initiatives to adapt its offer to the changing needs of its customers, particularly in the area of online banking and specialized banking services. This divestment is part of the Group's strategy to focus on its core activities and strengthen its position on the market.

title: "BNP Paribas: Results of the fourth quarter below expectations, impacted by extraordinary provisions"
content: BNP PARIBAS announces results for the fourth quarter below expectations, with a net share of €1.169 billion, down 50% from the €2.34 billion consensus. This is mainly due to extraordinary negative elements, including extraordinary provisions for disputes related to the mortgage portfolios of Personal Finance and Poland, which amount to €320 million and €221 million respectively. Gross operating income fell from 8.8% to €3.486 billion, while the risk cost increased from 39.4% to -972 million. Net banking income increased by 0.1% to 11.43 billion, but management costs increased by 4.2%. The bank announced a 2023 dividend of €4.81 in cash, up 18%, and a share buyback program of €1.05 billion in 2024. BNP PARIBAS's hard equity ratio (CET1) stood at 13.2%, up 90 basis points over the year. Regarding the outlook, the bank forecasts a return on tangible equity (ROTE) by 2025 between 11.5% and 12%, but targets a ROTE of 12% by 2026.

title: Oddo BHF is increasing its price target for KERING to €509.77, anticipating an organic growth of 8% by 2025.
content: Oddo BHF maintains a positive analysis on KERING with a price target adjusted to €509.77, reflecting an upward revision of the estimates and the more favourable market conditions. As a reminder, KERING published annual revenues of €17,194 million in 2024, showing growth that demonstrates its strong position on the market. EBIT 2024 stands at €1,133 million, with a margin of 6.6% that reflects a strong operational performance. Our initial revenue and EBIT 2024 forecasts were respectively €16,860 million and €1,050 million. In this context, Oddo BHF revises its organic growth forecast for 2025, expected to be 8%, and adjusts its EBIT forecasts accordingly, now anticipating an EBIT margin of 7.1% for 2025. This revision implies a 5% increase in EBIT forecasts for 2025.

title: Oddo BHF maintains a 'neutral' rating on Kering with a price target lowered to 412 euros.
content: Oddo BHF confirms its 'neutral' rating on the Kering title with a lower price target of 423 to 412 euros. Yesterday, the luxury group published a turnover of 2023 to 19 566 million euros, i.e. -2% in organic and an EBIT of 4 746 million euros, i.e. a margin of 24.3% (vs 27.5% for 2022). 'We estimate on 19,416 million euros and 4,843 million euros (margin to 24.9%) respectively', recalls Oddo BHF, which evokes a turnover in the 4th quarter 'ternal but under control', to -4% tcc. The analyst indicates that he has slightly adjusted his organic group growth forecast from 3.8% to 3.2% out of 2024. For 2025, our group turnover forecast reflects an organic growth of 6.8% (unchanged) with an EBIT margin of 23.4% (vs 25.2% prec). The broker reports that Kering is planning a decline in EBIT 2024 in view of the efforts undertaken to support and revive the brands and in particular Gucci that Oddo BHF considers 'always in transition'. Oddo BHF thus evokes a significant adjustment of EBIT forecasts to -8% for 2024 and -7% for 2025, while Kering's latest annual revenues were 19,566 million euros in 2023 and 17,194 million euros in 2024, with net profits of 2,983 million euros in 2023 and 1,133 million euros in 2024.

title: "VINCI shows results 2023 up sharply and optimizes its dividend"
content: VINCI (DG) has revealed results of 2023 with a sharp improvement. The construction and services group has a net profit of 4,863 billion euros compared to 4,702 billion euros in 2022. The current operating income increased from 10.2% to 7,278 billion euros, i.e. a current operating margin of 10% compared to 9.5% in 2022. The turnover increased by 4.2% on a perimeter comparable to 69,885 billion euros. The net cash flow of debt amounts to EUR 12.1 billion compared to EUR 9.5 billion at the end of 2022. The Board of Directors will propose at the annual general meeting to be held on June 5th to distribute a dividend for fiscal year 2023, reflecting the group's confidence in its ability to generate solid and stable cash flows. In view of the evolution of the market at the end of February, VINCI anticipates a moderate growth in its production of 2024, in line with the trends observed in 2023. The efforts to optimize the cost structure are continuing and are expected to contribute to an operating profitability maintained around 10% of the year's turnover.

title: The Court of Cassation confirms the sanction against Bloomberg in the case of the false press release VINCI.
content: The Court of Cassation rejected Bloomberg's appeal in the case of the false press release concerning VINCI (DG), our company. In November 2016, Bloomberg had issued incorrect information by relaying the content of a false press release concerning us, causing a fall in our price of 18.28 % before a partial recovery. This case had already resulted in a penalty of 5 million euros from the Autorité des marchés financiers (AMF), reduced to 3 million euros in 2021 by the Court of Appeal of Paris. The Court of Cassation confirmed this sanction, considering that Bloomberg had failed to comply with the rules of his profession by disseminating this information without sufficient verification. We assessed the damage suffered to investors to 6.5 million euros. Our closing price had finally dropped by 3.78% on that day. Our financial statements for the last few years show that our turnover has continued to grow (€50.23 billion in 2021, €62.51 billion in 2022, €69.88 billion in 2023 and €72.77 billion in 2024) despite this event. Our net result has also continued to grow (€2.597 billion in 2021, €4.259 billion in 2022, €4.702 billion in 2023 and €4.863 billion in 2024). We are pleased that this business is now closed and we are focusing on our business, with a current price of €175.12.

title: AXA reaches a new annual record at €730.00, towards an absolute record?
content: AXA (CS) is embedded in a 5-week bullish funicular: the title testes €730.00, new annual record and best brand since the record close of 13/02/23 (€729.50). AXA (CS) surpasses the zenith of €724.50 of 27/02/23 and will try to establish a new absolute record, beyond €735.00 (in intraday on 10 February 2022 and then €730.50 in closing).

title: "AXA plans to abolish 900 posts in France to reach €1.7 billion in savings by 2026"
content: Three days before the publication of its annual results, AXA (CS) unveiled a plan to reorganise its headquarters in France, which will result in the abolition of 900 posts, or about 5% of the headquarters' staff. On the Stock Exchange, AXA's share (CS) loses 2.01% to 880.42 euros. On the occasion of the presentation of its strategic plan in September, which had been welcomed, the Director General had announced around 1.7 billion euros of savings by 2026, including 700 million euros of additional savings. This reorganization project, which would "constitute an important step in the realization of the envisaged additional savings", aims to "simplify its operation and structurally improve its operational efficiency". AXA (CS) wants to "regroup and pool certain activities and functions" and "remove hierarchical layers to alleviate decision-making processes." These cost reductions should allow the operating coefficient - measures the operational efficiency of the company - to fall to less than 60% in 2026 against an operating coefficient 2023 of 94.1% ( 86.015.000.000 / 91.483.000.000 ). It specifies that it will deploy "all the accompanying measures in force in its social pact through internal mobilitys, transitions of activity or voluntary departures". The AXA Group employs almost 49,000 employees worldwide according to its annual report 2023 . This reorganization dossier is submitted today to the consultation of the bodies representing the staff, the outcome of which is planned in the second quarter of 2024. AXA (CS) will publish its annual results on Thursday, February 8.

title: SAFRAN and Exail Technologies join forces to revolutionize cab connectivity with the WIZY-WAP.
content: SAFRAN announces that it has signed a first partnership with Exail Technologies for the WIZY-WAP, the latest generation of on-board communications equipment, which is expected to generate an annual turnover of several million euros. Exail has selected SAFRAN to integrate this new solution into its cabin connectivity system for commercial aviation. WIZY-WAP will be integrated into both the new aircraft and the retrofit of SAFRAN's current fleet. This partnership is a new step in the development of SAFRAN's connectivity product range, which has already contributed to a significant increase in the company's turnover, from 15 133 million euros in 2021 to 23 651 million euros in 2023. This financial growth reflects the positive momentum of the group, which also returned to profits in 2023, showing a net profit of 3,444 million euros, after a loss of 2,459 million euros in 2022. This strategic partnership strengthens SAFRAN's position in the commercial aviation market and confirms its commitment to offer innovative and efficient solutions to its customers.

title: SAFRAN unscrews by 8.12% after disappointing guidance for 2024-2025!
content: SAFRAN's sharpest decline in the SBF120 index is from 8.12% to 496.99 euros after revealing its guidelines for the financial year 2024-2025. "These are very disappointing, again shifting the recovery of the market", comments Invest Securities. SAFRAN expects turnover to increase slightly by 2% at constant scope and exchange rates, to reach around 24 billion euros, "with a potential increase if aviation activity recovers earlier than expected and with a strong seasonality effect during the financial year." In detail, the turnover of the first half of the year is expected to decrease by about 5% at constant scope and exchange rates, with a low point expected in the 1st quarter of 2024-2025 due to delays in the delivery of certain components. The manufacturer of aeronautical components then anticipates a rebound in turnover in the second half of the year, in particular "covered by the end of the correction of the stocks in the foundries and a sustained commercial activity". Ebitda's margin for the financial year 2024-2025 is expected to be around 27%, down from that of the previous year. SAFRAN confirmed that it was waiting for the financial year 2023-2024 for a turnover increase of about 13% at constant scope and exchange rates, to reach about 23.65 billion euros, and an Ebitda margin of about 29%, according to the indications given on 7 February last, despite a net loss of 2.46 billion euros in 2022-2023. Results 2023-2024 will be published on 22 May next.

title: "Wellington Management Group exceeds 5% of Pernod Ricard's capital, a sign of confidence in the future of the company"
content: Wellington Management Group, acting on behalf of funds and customers, reported to the AMF that on 15 February 2024 it had crossed the 5% threshold of the capital of Pernod Ricard (RI), and held, on behalf of the said funds and customers, 5.05% of the capital and 4.22% of the voting rights of that company. This threshold was the result of an acquisition of shares Pernod Ricard (RI) in the market, reflecting investor confidence in the financial soundness and growth prospects of the company, which recently published solid results with a total turnover of 11.598 billion euros and a net profit of 1.476 billion euros as at 30 June 2024. This increased participation of Wellington Management Group reflects a positive vision of the future of Pernod Ricard (RI), whose shares are currently valued at 330.97 euros.

title: Pernod Ricard: revenue down 1% in the third quarter, financial targets adjusted downwards
content: Pernod Ricard announced a decrease of 1% in comparable figures for its third quarter, to €2.9 billion, a decrease of €116 million compared to the same period of the previous year. "The pressure on consumption in the Europe area, as well as in certain regions of America and Asia, led to a decrease in sales volumes during the period, particularly marked in the large distribution, both for our brands and for brands of distributors," the group said in a press release. In this context, Pernod Ricard adjusted its sales growth target for the year to a decrease of 2% to 3%, compared with an initial forecast of 5%. However, the group confirms its objective of increasing current operating income, which is expected to increase by about 10%, in line with market expectations. This downward revision of its financial targets reflects the challenges Pernod Ricard faces in a difficult economic environment.

title: DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE strengthens its cloud computing infrastructure in Île-de-France with a new region of availability.
content: DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE announces the launch of a new region of availability in Île-de-France, thus strengthening its cloud computing infrastructure to offer a robust presence in three geographically close data centers, guaranteeing resilience and low latency times. This strategic location, located near Paris, allows DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE to offer its customers a solution that allows them to benefit from replicated data for almost instantaneous or distributed changeovers for scale-up, thus providing increased resilience. This initiative is in line with our strategy to expand and diversify our cloud offerings, while new regions are expected to be deployed in the coming years. We are convinced that this development will allow us to better meet the needs of our customers and strengthen our position in the market. With a turnover of €6.21 billion in 2024 and a net profit of €1.20 billion, DASSAULT SYSTEMS SE has the resources to invest in strategic initiatives such as this one, thereby strengthening its leadership in the software and cloud computing sector.

title: "DSY collapses by 30% in 48 hours, investors worried about a possible ultra-dilutive capital increase"
content: Investors react very negatively to the 2 revised proposals for financial restructuring, from DSY associated with a number of investors on the one hand, and from a consortium composed of some current and potential shareholders on the other hand. Dassault Systèmes SE, which is deviating by -16%, sinks its 'absolute low' of €275.5 on 21 and 25 March and collapses from -30% in 48H, under €232, towards a new 'lower' of €218, which seems to integrate the prospect of an ultra-dilutive capital increase. And the session is not over: a split by 2 in less than 1 month compared to the recent zenith of €390/395 cannot be excluded, i.e. a target of €165.625/182.5, given that its net income has increased by 55% in 4 years, investors are worried that this title will reach such thresholds while its CA has increased by only 28% over the same period.

title: "UBS goes to 'neutral' on Stellantis and raises its target price to 300 euros"
content: UBS announced on Friday that it had moved to a 'neutral' opinion on the Stellantis title, against a 'sell' advice previously, with a price target raised from 31 to 50 euros, i.e. a target adjusted to 300 euros, on the basis of our internal growth projections, taking into account the current multiples of the sector. The broker explains that after the cancellation of some projects and the Brussels survey on Chinese electric cars, it envisages a 'interesting' redistribution of liquidity to shareholders. According to its calculations, Stellantis should be one of the groups in the sector to display a good financial dynamic this year, with revenues amounting to 156.878 billion euros in the last financial year. Note that the company's net profit amounted to 5.473 billion euros over the same period. "After a solid first quarter, we are more confident about Stellantis' ability to achieve its financial objectives this year," he says. "The broker's only reservation, according to him, is that some strategic directions - which do not aim at operational balance before next year - are holding back possible returns to shareholders. Stellantis' title was exchanged at 245.45 euros at the opening of the stock market session.

title: Deutsche Bank adjusts its price target on Stellantis to 32 euros, however confirming its purchase recommendation.
content: Deutsche Bank announced on Thursday that it had reduced its target price on STELLANTIS N.V. (STLAM) from 38 to 32 euros, while maintaining a recommendation for purchase on the title. In a research note, the intermediary states that it is expected that the car manufacturer, well known for controlling its costs, will begin to be affected by a number of difficulties, notably after publishing a net profit of 5.47 billion euros over the last 12 months, a significant decrease compared to 18.596 billion euros of the previous year. In his view, the fourth quarter performance, which will be published on 15 February, should thus be penalized by a less favourable 'mix' and by negative exchange rate effects, which are expected to weigh on the margins until the second half of the year, while turnover has also fallen from 189.544 billion euros last year to 156.878 billion euros over the last 12 months. However, with regard to its outlook for 2024, Deutsche Bank expects STELTANTIS N.V. (STLAM) to stick to its objective of a two-digit adjusted operating margin and a positive cash flow available in its industrial activities, despite the rise in wage costs, lower price effects and low sales volumes. The analyst believes that the builder will also maintain the broad outlines of his capital redistribution program for its shareholders.

title: Danone had a turnover of 27,376 billion euros in 2024, up 1.2%.
content: DANONE announces that it has achieved a total turnover increase of 1.2% to 27,376 billion euros in its financial year 2024, including a dairy product activity increasing from 1% to 20,21 billion euros, in line with the Group's expectations. Against the background of a slight decrease in volumes on the dairy products market, the Group's strategic repositioning on premium segments and the solid evolution of specialised nutrition activity have thus achieved a slight increase in turnover, even if the original ambition was to aim for a 5% growth. This performance is in line with the solid results of previous years, with a net income of 2,021 billion euros in 2024, thus strengthening the Group's confidence in its ability to create long-term value for its shareholders.

title: "Wellington Management Group reduces its stake in Danone to 4.99% of the capital"
content: Wellington Management Group LLP, acting on behalf of funds and customers, reported to the AMF that on June 18, it had crossed the 5% threshold of Danone's capital, as a result of a sale of shares on the market. The declarant specified that it held 12,769,809 Danone shares representing as many voting rights, i.e. 4,99% of the capital and 4,17% of the voting rights of the agri-food group, while the current price of Danone share is €337.02. This sale relates to a share of the capital which had contributed €881 million to Danone's net profit in the previous year, the total turnover of which was €27.62 billion.

title: HSBC raises its STMicroelectronics target to 98 euros, now its purchase recommendation.
content: HSBC announced on Monday that it had increased its target price on STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) from €90 to €98, while remaining on the title, after having met with the financial director of the electronic components manufacturer. The broker explained that he had noted his forecast results following this appointment, which, according to him, had highlighted a still strong demand for the Group's main markets, particularly in the automotive and consumer electronics sectors. From his point of view, the priority given by STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. to its profitability rather than sales volumes should allow him to improve its profit margins over the medium term, after having achieved a turnover of €13.27 billion in the last financial year and a net profit of €1.56 billion. In the longer term, the broker says he hopes that the group will risk revealing prospects for results by 2027 on its next day of investors. HSBC warns, however, that delays in the conduct of certain projects could compromise the group's margins over the second half of the year, but remains confident in the ability of STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. to maintain a positive growth path.

title: STMicroelectronics: Decline from 0.76% to 99.55 euros, impacted by the disappointing goals of Texas Instruments
content: In spite of the general trend of European technological values, STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) is down from 0.76% to 99.55 euros. The semiconductor manufacturer is penalized by the presentation yesterday evening by its American competitor, Texas Instruments, of disappointing targets. In the first quarter, STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) is planning a profit per share that may prove to be lower than the analysts' expectations, who anticipate a net profit in decline compared to the 4211000000.0 dollars of the previous year, an amount that could be below 1.42 dollars per share for revenues that could reach 13269000000.0 dollars. In the fourth quarter of the previous year, STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) achieved a net profit of 1557000000.0 dollars, or $1.49 dollars per share. Its sales declined to $13269000000.0. STMICROELECTRONICS N.V. (STMPA) CEO commented: "During the quarter, we experienced growing weakness in the industrial sector and a sequential decline in the automotive sector."

title: Crédit Agricole S.A. strengthens its presence in Belgium with the acquisition of 9% of Ageas for 730 million euros.
content: Crédit Agricole S.A. (ACA) announces that it has signed, through its insurance subsidiary Crédit Agricole Assurances, an agreement for the redemption of the stake of approximately 9% held by the Chinese group Fosun in the Belgian insurance company Ageas. A 4.8% of the capital will be acquired in the next few days, and the balance after the required regulatory authorisations. The total price, estimated at around 730 million euros, corresponds to a marginal impact on the CET1 ratio of about two basis points, which should be easily absorbed since Crédit Agricole S.A.'s CET1 is currently very solid. Crédit Agricole S.A. Recalled to benefit from a partnership with Ageas, through AG Insurance (AGI), which will result in an exclusive insurance distribution agreement between AGI and Crédit Agricole, and a significant participation in AGI, thus strengthening the close links between the two groups. This strategic acquisition will enable Crédit Agricole S.A. to strengthen its presence in the Belgian insurance market and diversify its activities. With a net profit of 7,087 billion euros in 2024 and a total revenue of 25,328 billion euros, Crédit Agricole S.A. is well positioned to integrate this new participation and benefit from it.

title: "Crédit Agricole : The results disappoint, the course of the action under pressure"
content: CREDIT AGRICOLE S.A. (ACA): The French banking group also experienced a decline last Thursday due to results below expectations. The peaks are no longer on the rise. Given that the current price of the share is 399,42 €, the recent downside gap suggests that an area around 360 € could be tested, which is worrying given the latest annual revenues which, although increasing, may not be sufficient to support the course of the action.

title: The European Stock Exchanges in dispersed order, investors are waiting for the results of S&P500 and economic forecasts.
content: The European Stock Exchanges are evolving in order dispersed this Thursday (+0.6% in London, but -0.6% in Frankfurt and Paris), for a session under the sign of the publication of company results for the first three months of the year. In this context, investors look forward to the publication of the results of S&P500, which could influence the market. Previous publications of some companies have received mixed receptions: some have seen their action increase, while others have been sanctioned. Note that the economic forecasts, notably the publication of the US GDP for the first quarter, are also expected to have an impact on the markets. Finally, investors are closely monitoring the evolution of the capital expenditure of technology companies, which could have implications for their future growth, as some companies that have recently revised their spending forecasts upwards, as well as supporting their AI roadmap. Overall, markets appear to evolve in a tempting climate pending new information that could influence short-term trends, particularly as regards S&P500.

title: "S&P500 reaffirms its ethical commitment after serious allegations in the sector"
content: We inform shareholders that serious allegations have been made against a company in the sector, recalling the importance of our commitment to responsible practices. A case similar to the one recently brought to the attention of the public concerning a service delivery company, currently judged in Châlons-en-Champagne, highlights the risks associated with the treatment of workers, particularly in those sectors where the temporary labour force is sought. According to recent information, some 50 illegal foreign workers have been accommodated under unworthy conditions by a service delivery company similar to those with which we collaborate. The conditions of accommodation, including mattresses on the ground, showers of fortune with very little hot water and a defective electrical installation, have been reported, and these allegations are currently under investigation and will be examined by the court. Charges, including trafficking in human beings, unworthy accommodation conditions, hidden work and the employment of employees without work title, are taken very seriously. As a responsible company, S&P500 reaffirms its commitment to respect human rights and to guarantee decent working conditions for all its employees and partners. The current course of our action is 172,96 €. We take these allegations very seriously and strengthen our procedures to prevent such situations in our value chain. We will continue to follow this matter closely and inform you of any significant development. Our priority remains the protection of our reputation, the well-being of our employees and the maintenance of the trust of our shareholders.

title: "Dow Jones Industrial Average: a remarkable increase of 22% in 6 months, optimistic investors"
content: Dow Jones Industrial Average has shown strong results, well above expectations, demonstrating positive market dynamics. For the past six months, the Dow Jones Industrial Average has recorded a remarkable increase of 22%, a trend that is in line with an encouraging upward trend. Investors are particularly active, helping to exceed the levels of resistance observed over the last two years. Recently, prices have found a solid support at €140.45, and the next goals are beyond the current resistance, to be followed to assess the potential for future growth.

title: The Dow Jones index fell to 230.49 points in June 2024, a decline that worries investors.
content: In June 2024, Dow Jones Industrial Average's summary business climate index stood at 230.49 points, according to the company's board of directors at 8.45 a.m. For example, investor morale decreased by 1.5 points over a month, resulting in heavy losses. At this level, the index is below its benchmark average, investors' opinion on the evolution of the economic situation is rather negative. In May, the index was 232.0 points (a figure just reviewed by the board of directors), up 0.5 points over a month, but unfortunately this trend did not continue.

title: CAC40: Investor Optimism Boosted by Remarkable Increase and Solid Prospects
content: In a favourable market environment, CAC40's shares have increased by almost 0.9%, reaching a remarkable level of € 182.05, thanks to a positive trend that characterizes the stock exchange day. This performance is part of an environment where investors are attentive to the results of companies. The asset manager Pictet AM recently noted its recommendation on CAC40's "neutral" actions to "overweight", considering that they are increasingly attractive. This decision reflects renewed confidence in the company's growth and return potential. The re-acceleration of economic activity should allow CAC40 to exceed the expectations of results established by the consensus this year. Investors are optimistic about the company's ability to take advantage of this positive trend. In the context of the few economic indicators available, industrial production in Europe has shown signs of moderation. However, the outlook for CAC40 remains strong, given its market position and its ability to generate positive results. In the bond sector, the trend towards lower rates is continuing, which could benefit CAC40 for future issues or refinancing. Finally, in the news of companies in the same sector, the announcements of solid results and strategic decisions, such as those of Alstom and Michelin, show a positive dynamic that could have a positive impact on CAC40.

title: CAC40 in freefall: Opep+ disappoints investors with its decision on oil production
content: The shares of CAC40 fell on Monday, with a loss of 3.39% to 165.13 €, after the announcement of the gradual end of certain production restrictions of the Opep+ as early as October, which disappointed investors. This decision led to increased uncertainty about the prospects of demand, weighing on the markets. For analysts, this decrease is linked to the meeting of the Opep+ the day before, which decided to extend the current production reductions until the end of September before gradually putting barrels back on the market in the following 12 months. As early as October 2024, the Opep+ will end one of its production reduction strata. Investors are concerned about the abundance of the supply that might result from this decision, and Goldman Sachs described it as a "bassier" for the market. DNB analysts even believe that the group of exporting countries will have to mourn the CAC40 action beyond €165 "if the alliance acts as planned", not seeing "no room for additional CAC40 actions on the market". The current Opep+ production reductions total slightly less than 6 mbj and are all renewed at least until September. However, with the end of the additional voluntary reductions and the increase in the production target of some members, Opep+ could reintroduce 2.5 mbj from September 2024 to September 2025. However, the group recalled that it retained the possibility of stopping or even reversing the exit of one of its production reduction strata if market conditions deteriorate, understand whether prices are falling. In this context, CAC40's shareholders are invited to closely monitor market developments.

title: The S&P GSCI Gold Index is growing moderately in a favourable economic environment
content: The positive trend in financial markets continues, and investors continue to follow with interest the evolution of the company S&P GSCI Gold Index, currently at €198.98. In a context where markets are attentive to economic indicators, particularly those related to inflation and central bank decisions, the S&P GSCI Gold Index has benefited from a favourable market environment. Investors remain focused on interest rate prospects, which could influence investment decisions in the sector. 'We are still expecting an increase in gold-related assets by the end of the year, but it is likely that the next few months will follow a moderate trend', the analysts point out. The PMI index of overall activity in the euro area reported an acceleration of private sector growth, although at a moderate pace, but the most marked for almost a year. This consolidation of the recovery marks the end of the prolonged period of weakness recorded throughout the second half of 2023. Industrial production prices fell by 0.4% in the euro area in March compared to the previous month. Bond markets are continuing to improve. The dollar is still declining and the euro is symmetrically appreciating by +0.2% to $1.077/E, the ounce of gold recovers by +0.9% to $2323 per ounce. In this context, investors continue to be interested in S&P GSCI Gold Index. This week, the focus will be on the University of Michigan's consumer confidence index, which will allow us to better understand the current morale of American households on Friday. J. Safra Sarasin's teams believe that it is likely that the next few months will follow the usual seasonal pattern with only a moderate increase in gold-related assets.

title: Gold in decline: Investors withdraw in the face of economic uncertainty.
content: Investors seem to be withdrawing from gold positions, reflecting a downward trend in European markets this Thursday. The price of the S&P GSCI Gold Index share is declining, currently at €179.22, in a context where precious metals are sensitive to global economic conditions. Yesterday, the US markets recorded solid gains following the publication of inflation figures, which were found to be rather satisfactory. The rise in consumer prices in the US slowed in April to 3.4% in gross data and 3.6% in underlying data. However, Commerzbank analysts estimate that although inflationary pressures have eased, they remain too high. This may influence the Fed's decisions on interest rates, but it is unlikely that immediate action will be taken. Today, a number of important macroeconomic data are to be published in the United States, including figures for industrial production and residential construction, as well as quarterly results from large companies. In this uncertain context, investors may remain cautious in their investment decisions in the precious metals sector, including for the S&P GSCI Gold Index. Future financial publications could further influence the market trend and the course of action of the company.
